meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve can't deploy apps (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't deploy apps. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Project Template to Solve Deployment
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with deployment. Use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to launch your first student SaaS app with expert guidance.
content.answer_block: Deployment failure usually stems from environment configuration mismatches. By using the Antigravity project template, you standardize your build pipeline, ensuring local code matches production environments perfectly. Combine this with Claude Code’s automated debugging to bridge the gap between your local prototype and a live, scalable web application.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks hunched over your keyboard, crafting the perfect logic for your first SaaS project. The code runs beautifully on 'localhost,' but the moment you attempt to push it to the public, the screen fills with cryptic red errors and broken dependencies. This is the 'Deployment Wall'—a rite of passage for every developer that kills most student projects before they ever see the light of day.

But what if you could bypass the infrastructure headache entirely? The Antigravity project template is designed to abstract away the complexity of server configuration, letting you focus on feature building rather than DevOps. By pairing this with the precision of Claude Code, you turn your laptop into a high-octane production engine, transforming your 'it works on my machine' nightmare into a deployed, professional-grade application.

steps.title: Initialize with Antigravity
steps.description: Start by cloning the baseline template which comes pre-configured with industry-standard deployment hooks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the standard web-app template with TypeScript and Vercel deployment configuration.

steps.title: Configure Environment Variables
steps.description: Isolate your API keys and secrets to ensure the build pipeline remains secure and portable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure .env.example file and provide a script to validate required environment variables before build.

steps.title: Automate Dependency Resolution
steps.description: Use Claude Code to scan your current imports and ensure they are compatible with the target runtime environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit my current dependency tree for any version conflicts that might break during a production build.

steps.title: Implement Production Build Hooks
steps.description: Define the build steps that Antigravity will execute to transform your source code into deployable static assets.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add a build-check script that runs linting and unit tests before the final deployment bundle is generated.

steps.title: Verify Deployment Readiness
steps.description: Run a final simulation of the deployment process to identify potential bottlenecks before the official push.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Simulate the deployment process and identify any missing build environment configurations.

pro_tips: Always use a .env.example file; never commit real secrets to your repo, as public repositories are scanned by bots within seconds.
Keep your build steps deterministic by pinning versions in your package-lock.json file to avoid 'works today, fails tomorrow' syndrome.
Utilize Claude Code to write unit tests for your deployment scripts, not just your application logic.
If your project hits a memory limit during build, use Antigravity's lazy-loading components to reduce the initial bundle size.
Always check your 'dist' or 'build' folder locally before pushing to verify the compiled output matches your intent.
content.next_steps: Once your project is live, focus on setting up a custom domain and integrating analytics to monitor how real users interact with your first build.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting deployment errors hold your portfolio back. With the right template and the right AI tools, you are minutes away from your first successful launch. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
